# simulate_admixture { #tspaint.simulate_admixture }

```python
tspaint.simulate_admixture(
    demography,
    *,
    n_query=10,
    n_reference=10,
    samples=None,
    sequence_length=1000000.0,
    recombination_rate=1e-08,
    ploidy=2,
    mutation_rate=None,
    random_seed=42,
    validate=True,
)
```

Simulate an admixture from a **role-tagged demography**, returning a :class:`Simulation`.

Pass any :class:`msprime.Demography` whose populations you have tagged with :func:`pop_role`
(query / source / reference / ghost + ancestry state) — either hand-built for full control, or
one of the :func:`admixture_demography` presets. This samples the query and reference panels,
runs the coalescent, optionally lays down mutations, and packages the queries, reference
``labels`` and the census ``truth`` (ghost tracts embedded); the caller never touches a
population-name constant.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**demography**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[msprime](`msprime`).[Demography](`msprime.Demography`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A role-tagged demography — hand-built with :func:`pop_role` (must include an ``add_census`` just older than the admixture pulse), or from an :func:`admixture_demography` preset.

<code>[**n_query**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [10]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Number of query (admixed) individuals to sample.

<code>[**n_reference**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [10]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Number of individuals sampled from **each** reference / source population.

<code>[**samples**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Explicit ``{population_name: n_individuals}`` sampling, overriding ``n_query`` / ``n_reference`` — used by the variant wrappers to sample e.g. unequal pure / impure panels.

<code>[**sequence_length**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1000000.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Passed to :func:`msprime.sim_ancestry`.

<code>[**recombination_rate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1000000.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Passed to :func:`msprime.sim_ancestry`.

<code>[**ploidy**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1000000.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Passed to :func:`msprime.sim_ancestry`.

<code>[**random_seed**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [1000000.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Passed to :func:`msprime.sim_ancestry`.

<code>[**mutation_rate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If given, lay down mutations at this per-base rate (:func:`tspaint.io.add_mutations`); the returned ``ts`` then carries sites. ``None`` (default) leaves the bare ancestry.

<code>[**validate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[bool](`bool`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Run :func:`check_admixture_contract` first (default ``True``): raise a clear error on a missing census, no query, a state-less source, a mis-combined ghost, or an untagged census-bearing population, instead of silently producing wrong truth. Set ``False`` to skip.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Simulation](`tspaint.sim.Simulation`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``.ts`` (mutated iff ``mutation_rate`` given), ``.queries``, ``.labels``, ``.truth_states`` (ghost tracts embedded with a state above the sources), ``.sample_sets``, ``.ghost_states`` (the ghost-only truth) and ``.source_states`` (the paintable state indices).

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Two-source preset:

```python
>>> sim = simulate_admixture(admixture_demography(), n_query=5, n_reference=5,
...                          sequence_length=1e5, random_seed=1)
>>> sim.ts.num_samples
30
```

Hand-built: three sampled sources A/B/C + one unsampled ghost, painted at ``K=3`` while
:func:`tspaint.detect_ghost` finds the ghost; the ghost shows in the truth band of the plot:

```python
>>> import msprime, tspaint
>>> from tspaint.sim import pop_role, simulate_admixture
>>> Ne = 2000
>>> d = msprime.Demography()
>>> for i, n in enumerate("ABC"):
...     d.add_population(name=n, initial_size=Ne,
...                      extra_metadata=pop_role(state=i, source=True, reference=True))
>>> d.add_population(name="GHOST", initial_size=Ne, extra_metadata=pop_role(ghost=True))
>>> d.add_population(name="ADMIX", initial_size=Ne, extra_metadata=pop_role(query=True))
>>> for n in ("AB", "ANC", "ROOT"):
...     d.add_population(name=n, initial_size=Ne)
>>> d.add_admixture(time=100, derived="ADMIX", ancestral=["A", "B", "C"], proportions=[.3, .3, .4])
>>> d.add_mass_migration(time=90, source="ADMIX", dest="GHOST", proportion=0.05)
>>> d.add_census(time=101.0)
>>> d.add_population_split(time=2000, derived=["A", "B"], ancestral="AB")
>>> d.add_population_split(time=6000, derived=["AB", "C"], ancestral="ANC")
>>> d.add_population_split(time=20000, derived=["ANC", "GHOST"], ancestral="ROOT")
>>> d.sort_events()                       # events added out of time order -> sort before simulating
>>> sim = simulate_admixture(d, n_query=8, n_reference=6, sequence_length=1e6)
>>> painting = tspaint.paint(sim.ts, sim.labels, sim.queries, K=len(sim.source_states))
```
